description: Full export-device-metrics workflow: find device by name → list datasources → list instances → get data (4 steps)

steps.userPrompt: I need to export CPU metrics from our production web server called 'web-prod-01'. First, find that device so we know its ID.
steps.syntheticResult: Found device: ID 750, 'web-prod-01' (IP: 10.0.1.50, hostStatus: alive, preferredCollectorId: 12). Stored in session as lastDevice, lastDeviceId=750.

steps.userPrompt: Good, the device ID is 750. Now show me what datasources are available on it. I'm looking for CPU-related ones.
steps.syntheticResult: Listed 8 datasources for device 750. Matching CPU filter: [{ id: 101, name: 'WinCPU', monitoringInstanceNumber: 4 }, { id: 102, name: 'CPUBusyPercent', monitoringInstanceNumber: 1 }]. Stored in session.

steps.userPrompt: Let's look at the instances for the WinCPU datasource (ID 101) on that device.
steps.syntheticResult: Listed 4 instances for datasource 101 on device 750: [{ id: 1, name: 'CPU-0' }, { id: 2, name: 'CPU-1' }, { id: 3, name: 'CPU-2' }, { id: 4, name: 'CPU-3' }]. Stored in session.

steps.userPrompt: Now pull the actual performance data for all 4 CPU instances over the last 24 hours.

description: Full batch-device-update workflow: list → review → batch update → verify (4 steps)

steps.userPrompt: I need to disable alerting on all devices in our staging environment. The staging devices all have 'staging' in their display name. Let's start by finding them.
steps.syntheticResult: Listed 8 devices matching filter. Session variables created: lastDeviceList (8 devices), lastDeviceListIds ([201,202,203,204,205,206,207,208]). Devices: staging-web-01, staging-web-02, staging-db-01, staging-db-02, staging-app-01, staging-app-02, staging-cache-01, staging-lb-01.

steps.userPrompt: Those look right — all 8 staging servers. Go ahead and disable alerting on all of them using the session variable.
steps.syntheticResult: Batch update completed: 8/8 succeeded. All devices now have disableAlerting=true. Session variables updated: lastUpdatedDevices.

steps.userPrompt: Great, now let's verify the changes took effect. List those staging devices again and show me their alerting status.

description: Full alert-triage workflow: list critical → get details → ack → add note (4 steps)

steps.userPrompt: We're getting paged about critical alerts. Show me all the critical severity alerts right now.
steps.syntheticResult:
Listed 3 critical alerts:
- Alert LM5001: 'CPU Critical' on web-prod-01 (started 2 hours ago)
- Alert LM5002: 'Disk Space Critical' on db-prod-01 (started 30 minutes ago)
- Alert LM5003: 'Memory Critical' on app-prod-03 (started 5 minutes ago)
Session variables: lastAlertList, lastAlertListIds (["LM5001","LM5002","LM5003"]).

steps.userPrompt: The disk space alert on db-prod-01 looks most urgent. Get me the full details on alert LM5002.
steps.syntheticResult: Alert LM5002 details: Disk Space Critical on db-prod-01. DataPoint: PercentUsed = 97.3%. Threshold: > 95%. Instance: /dev/sda1. Resource: db-prod-01 (ID: 500). Started: 30 minutes ago. Severity: Critical (4). Not yet acknowledged.

steps.userPrompt: I'm going to look into this now. Acknowledge that alert with a comment saying I'm investigating the disk space issue.
steps.syntheticResult: Alert LM5002 acknowledged successfully with comment.

steps.userPrompt: Also add a note to that same alert saying we're planning to expand the disk partition tonight during the maintenance window.

description: Full collector-health-check workflow: list collectors → identify issues → review groups (3 steps)

steps.userPrompt: I want to do a health check on all our collectors. Show me the full list with their status and device counts.
steps.syntheticResult:
Listed 6 collectors:
- ID 1: 'collector-east-01' status=up, numberOfHosts=245, build=34.100
- ID 2: 'collector-east-02' status=up, numberOfHosts=312, build=34.100
- ID 3: 'collector-west-01' status=up, numberOfHosts=198, build=34.050
- ID 4: 'collector-west-02' status=down, numberOfHosts=0, build=33.900
- ID 5: 'collector-dr-01' status=up, numberOfHosts=52, build=34.100
- ID 6: 'collector-dr-02' status=up, numberOfHosts=48, build=34.100
Session: lastCollectorList, lastCollectorListIds.

steps.userPrompt: Looks like collector-west-02 is down. Let's also check the collector groups to see how they're organized and if auto-balancing is configured.
steps.syntheticResult:
Listed 3 collector groups:
- ID 10: 'East Coast' numOfCollectors=2, autoBalance=true, threshold=500
- ID 11: 'West Coast' numOfCollectors=2, autoBalance=false
- ID 12: 'DR Site' numOfCollectors=2, autoBalance=true, threshold=200
Session: lastCollectorGroupList.

steps.userPrompt: The West Coast group has auto-balance disabled and one of its collectors is down. Let's enable auto-balance on that group. The group ID is 11.

description: Full user-audit workflow: list users → inspect suspicious account → suspend it (3 steps)

steps.userPrompt: I need to audit our LogicMonitor user accounts. List all users and show me their names, roles, and last login info.
steps.syntheticResult:
Listed 12 users:
- ID 1: admin@company.com (Administrator, lastLogin: 2026-03-22)
- ID 2: jdoe@company.com (Administrator, lastLogin: 2025-06-15)
- ID 3: asmith@company.com (Readonly, lastLogin: 2026-03-21)
- ID 55: old-api-user (API-only, lastLogin: 2024-01-10)
- ID 56: temp-contractor (Readonly, lastLogin: 2025-02-28)
... and 7 more active users.
Session: lastUserList, lastUserListIds.

steps.userPrompt: User 55 'old-api-user' hasn't logged in since January 2024, over two years ago. Let me see the full details on that account.
steps.syntheticResult: User 55 details: username='old-api-user', email='api-legacy@company.com', apionly=true, status='active', roles=[{id: 5, name: 'API-Only'}], lastLogin: 2024-01-10, note: 'Created for legacy integration'. No recent API activity detected.

steps.userPrompt: That account is definitely stale. Suspend it and add a note that it was disabled during the March 2026 security audit.

description: Full dashboard-clone workflow: find source → get config → create clone (3 steps)

steps.userPrompt: I want to clone our Production Overview dashboard to create a Staging Overview. First find the dashboard — it should have 'Production Overview' in the name.
steps.syntheticResult:
Found 1 dashboard matching filter:
- ID 42: 'Production Overview' (groupId: 5, 12 widgets, sharable: true)
Session: lastDashboardList.

steps.userPrompt: That's the one. Get the full configuration for dashboard 42 so we can clone it.
steps.syntheticResult: Dashboard 42 full config retrieved: name='Production Overview', groupId=5, description='Main production monitoring dashboard', widgetsConfig={...12 widgets...}, widgetTokens=[{name:'##defaultDeviceGroup##', value:'Production'}], sharable=true. Stored in session as lastDashboard.

steps.userPrompt: Now create the clone. Name it 'Staging Overview', put it in dashboard group 8, and keep the same widget configuration.

description: Complex cross-tool incident workflow: check alerts → find affected device → check metrics → disable alerting during fix (4 steps)

steps.userPrompt: We're seeing high CPU alerts on production. Show me all current critical alerts related to CPU.
steps.syntheticResult:
Listed 2 critical CPU alerts:
- Alert LM8001: 'CPU Critical' on device 'app-prod-05' (deviceId: 850), severity: 4, value: 98.5%
- Alert LM8002: 'CPU Critical' on device 'app-prod-06' (deviceId: 851), severity: 4, value: 96.2%
Session: lastAlertList.

steps.userPrompt: Let's look at device 850 (app-prod-05) to understand what's going on. Get the device details.
steps.syntheticResult: Device 850: 'app-prod-05', IP: 10.0.5.50, hostStatus: alive, deviceType: 6, preferredCollectorId: 2, hostGroupIds: [15, 42], customProperties: [{name:'env', value:'production'}, {name:'team', value:'platform'}]. Stored in session as lastDevice.

steps.userPrompt: Pull the CPU metrics for that device so we can see the trend. Check the datasources first.
steps.syntheticResult: Listed 15 datasources for device 850. CPU-related: [{id: 200, name: 'WinCPU', monitoringInstanceNumber: 8}, {id: 201, name: 'CPUBusyPercent', monitoringInstanceNumber: 1}]. Stored in session.

steps.userPrompt: We're going to do a rolling restart on that server. Temporarily disable alerting on device 850 so we don't get paged during the fix.

description: Large list produces compact response, LLM retrieves full details from session

steps.userPrompt: List all the devices in our environment
steps.syntheticResult: LogicMonitor device list succeeded. Returned 50 of 156 items. Session keys: lastDeviceList, lastDeviceListIds. Full item details available via: lm_session get key="lastDeviceList" fields="id,displayName" or index=N.

steps.userPrompt: I need the full details for the 5th device in that list. Use the session to retrieve it.

description: Full device SDT workflow: find device → create SDT → verify SDT is active (3 steps)

steps.userPrompt: I need to put our production database server 'db-prod-01' into a maintenance window. First, find that device.
steps.syntheticResult: Found device: ID 500, 'db-prod-01' (IP: 10.0.2.10, hostStatus: alive). Stored in session as lastDevice, lastDeviceId=500.

steps.userPrompt: Good, device ID is 500. Now create a one-time SDT on that device for the next 4 hours. Add a comment saying 'Database maintenance - index rebuild'.
steps.syntheticResult: SDT created successfully: ID 'R_88', type: ResourceSDT, deviceId: 500, sdtType: oneTime, isEffective: true, comment: 'Database maintenance - index rebuild'. Stored in session.

steps.userPrompt: Now let's verify the SDT is active. List all currently effective SDTs.
